TOEFL_iBT.frequency: every 1-2 weeks at test centers, any day for Home Edition

GRE.frequency: every 21 days, max 5 tests in 12 months

IELTS.scoring: each section 1-9, overall is average rounded to 0.5

reading.diagnostic: First always run one official practice test, then analyze error types by category (vocabulary/inference/detail/tone/insert-text/purpose)
reading.skim_method: Read first + last sentence of each paragraph only. Time limit: 2 min per passage.
reading.inference_drills: Practice 10 inference questions/day for 2 weeks. Goal: stop reading every word, start reading for structure.
reading.vocabulary: 800 high-frequency collocations > 5000-word list. Sources: Magoosh Vocabulary, official TOEFL reading passages.

listening.note_taking: Don't write everything. Write structure markers (contrast, example, opinion shift) + key nouns only.
listening.lecture_pattern: Most lectures follow: claim -> evidence -> counterclaim -> conclusion. Find the claim first.
listening.practice_ratio: Official TOEFL practice > third-party materials. TOEFL iBT has specific cadence.
listening.speed: 1.25x speed for first 2 weeks to build processing speed, then 1.0x for real practice.

writing.template_warning: Templates cap score at 6.5 IELTS / 24 TOEFL. No exceptions.
writing.task_1_academic: Overview (2 sentences) -> most significant feature -> second feature -> (sometimes) anomalies
writing.task_2_independent: State stance in intro. 2 body paragraphs, each: claim + specific example + explain why it supports stance. Conclusion restates.
writing.score_unlock_7: Original structure + specific examples + clear stance on every sentence

speaking.memorized_answer_penalty: Examiners detect rehearsed answers by intonation. Follow-up questions break them every time.
speaking.task_1_framework: Answer in 1 sentence -> reason in 2 sentences -> specific example in 2 sentences. Don't ramble.
speaking.task_2_3_strategy: Take notes as lecture plays. Capture 3-4 key points with their examples. Don't try to memorize.
speaking.fluency > accuracy: Small grammar errors are fine. Long pauses (>2 seconds) are death.

student_archetypes.behavior: Flashcards 200 words/day, practice test every week
student_archetypes.root_cause: Knows words, doesn't understand structure. Finishes passages with 5 minutes left, still gets inference questions wrong.
student_archetypes.fix: Stop memorizing, start skimming. Drill inference only for 2 weeks.

student_archetypes.behavior: Finishes with 10 minutes to spare, thinks speed is the goal
student_archetypes.root_cause: Careless errors. Misses 'except' / 'not' / 'least' in question stems.
student_archetypes.fix: Write every step. Slow down by 2 min/section. Error count drops 80%.

student_archetypes.behavior: Has essay templates for every possible prompt. Memorized 50+ sentence frames.
student_archetypes.root_cause: Examiners see templates 1000 times/year. They know the score ceiling.
student_archetypes.fix: Write 10 essays with zero templates. Essays 1-3 suck. Essay 7 breaks through.

student_archetypes.behavior: Taken test 4+ times. Score hasn't moved in 6 months.
student_archetypes.stuck_at: Whatever their current score is. Usually 10+ points below goal.
student_archetypes.root_cause: Practicing the same way over and over. Not analyzing what's wrong.
student_archetypes.fix: Stop taking full tests. Diagnose one weakness. Fix it. Then diagnose the next.

wrong_answer_traps: Too broad (technically true, not supported by passage)
Irrelevant (true in real world, not in passage)
toefl_reading_inference.key_skill: Find the logical link between two sentences the author didn't explicitly connect.

gre_text_completion.strategy: Don't read options first. Predict the blank from context clues. Then find closest option.

ielts_writing_band_7.band_7_threshold: Addresses all parts, clear position throughout, some rare errors allowed, good range of structures
